name: claude-hooks-posttooluse-bridge
tags: PostToolUse, bridge nodes, ActivateTaskNode, DeactivateTaskNode, DecisionTaskNode, checkpoint, task lifecycle, conditional, backfill, BackfillMemoryFilesNode, pluggable, execution_contract, execution philosophy
body:
PostToolUse conditional bridge nodes react to specific MCP tool calls and write to the LangGraph checkpoint:

- tasks__set_active / tasks__pop_active  → ActivateTaskNode → (conditional) BackfillMemoryFilesNode
  ActivateTaskNode writes active_task_id, task_memories, task_files, active_task_domain, execution_contract to checkpoint.
  execution_contract (task:380140fa, 2026-07-05) is a fixed north-star template string built by _build_execution_contract(task_id, title) — rendered verbatim into the system prompt every turn while the task is active, deliberately exempt from _enforce_context_budget eviction and _TASK_BODY_CHAR_CAP truncation (see [[claude-hooks-context-token-budget]]).
  If task_files is non-empty, BackfillMemoryFilesNode runs after — backfills memories.files=NULL for
  domain-matched memories using stem-token overlap (LIMIT 5 per activation).
  BackfillMemoryFilesNode is the default BackfillNodeProtocol slot — swappable by rewiring the graph.
  Skipped for replay-* and test-* sessions.
- tasks__clear_active / tasks__finish    → DeactivateTaskNode — zeros active task in checkpoint, including execution_contract
- tasks__add_decision                    → DecisionTaskNode   — appends to mid_task_decisions in checkpoint

Rule: MCP tools never write to checkpoint directly — bridge nodes are the only path.
